Making new patient characteristic table (table 1), comparing between repeater type 

# Libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
```

# Data 
```{r}
# First trying with Illinois only 
states <- c("IL")

all_states <- vector("list", length = length(states))
for(st in seq_along(states)){
  
  save_name <- paste0("tab1_chars_", states[[st]], ".csv")
  state <- read_csv(here("Trunk", "Derived", "new_patient_char", save_name))
  
  all_states[[st]] <- state
}

all_states <- bind_rows(all_states)
```

# Lists 
```{r}
# Diagnosis SMI 
DGNS_CD_SCZ <- c('F200', 'F201', 'F202', 'F203', 'F205', 'F2081', 'F2089', 'F209', 'F250', 'F251', 'F258', 'F259')

DGNS_CD_BD <- c('F3010','F3011','F3012','F3013','F302','F303','F304','F308','F309','F310','F3110','F3111','F3112','F3113','F312','F3130','F3131','F3132','F314','F315','F3160','F3161','F3162','F3163','F3164','F3170','F3171','F3172','F3173','F3174','F3175','F3176','F3177','F3178','F3181','F3189','F319')

DGNS_CD_MDD <- c('F320','F321','F322','F323','F324','F325','F329','F330','F331','F332','F333','F3340','F3341','F3342','F339','F341')

DGNS_CD_PSYCH <- c('F060', 'F061', 'F062', 'F0630', 'F0631', 'F0632', 'F0633', 'F0634', 'F064', 'F0670', 'F0671', 'F068', 'F22', 'F23', 'F24', 'F28', 'F29', 'F302', 'F312', 'F315', 'F3164', 'F323', 'F333', 'F4489')

smi <- c(DGNS_CD_BD, DGNS_CD_MDD, DGNS_CD_SCZ, DGNS_CD_PSYCH)

code_zero <- grep("0$", smi, value = TRUE)
code_nozero <- sub("0$", "", code_zero)

smi_code <- unique(c(smi, code_nozero))

smi_code <- gsub("\\.", "", smi_code)

# Diagnosis SUD 
# ICD-10 codes F10-F19 (this is the same way the SUD_DGNS_IND is coded in TAF)
sud_list <- paste0("F", 10:19)

```


# Making the table 
```{r}
check <- all_states %>% 
  # Check for the first visit diagnosis type 
  mutate(visit1_SUD = if_else(substr(visit1_dgns_1, 1, 3) %in% sud_list | substr(visit1_dgns_2, 1, 3) %in% sud_list, 1, 0),
         visit1_SMI = if_else(visit1_dgns_1 %in% smi_code | visit1_dgns_2 %in% smi_code, 1, 0)) %>% 
  mutate(
    spell_length = ifelse(repeat_visits == 0, "One Visit", "More Than One Visit"),
    spell_length = factor(spell_length, levels = c("One Visit", "More Than One Visit")),

    # Age groups
    age_group = case_when(
      AGE >= 13 & AGE <= 17 ~ "13-17",
      AGE >= 18 & AGE <= 25 ~ "18-25",
      AGE >= 26 & AGE <= 45 ~ "26-45",
      AGE >= 46 & AGE <= 62 ~ "46-62",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    age_group = factor(age_group, levels = c("13-17", "18-25", "26-45", "46-62")),

    # Sex
    sex_label = ifelse(SEX_CD == "F", "Female", "Male"),

    # Binary flags — adjust the values if yours differ
    visit1_SMI = as.numeric(visit1_SMI),
    visit1_SUD = as.numeric(visit1_SUD),
    last_year_bh_ip  = as.numeric(last_year_bh_ip),
    last_year_bh_ed  = as.numeric(last_year_bh_ed),
    last_year_any_ed = as.numeric(last_year_any_ed)
  )

# Create functions 
# For catergorical variables
cat_row <- function(data, var, level, label){
  data %>% 
    group_by(spell_length) %>% 
    summarise(n = sum(.data[[var]] == level, na.rm = TRUE),
              pct = mean(.data[[var]] == level, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
              .groups = "drop") %>% 
    mutate(cell = sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", n, pct)) %>%
    select(spell_length, cell) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = spell_length, values_from = cell) %>%
    mutate(Category = "", Variable = label) %>%
    select(Category, Variable, `One Visit`, `More Than One Visit`)
}

# For binary variables 
bin_row <- function(data, var, label) {
  data %>%
    group_by(spell_length) %>%
    summarise(
      n   = sum(.data[[var]] == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct = mean(.data[[var]] == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(cell = sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", n, pct)) %>%
    select(spell_length, cell) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = spell_length, values_from = cell) %>%
    mutate(Category = "", Variable = label) %>%
    select(Category, Variable, `One Visit`, `More Than One Visit`)
}

# This is done differently because the denominator of the percentage is different 
# number of patients with one visit / total number of new patients 
total_row <- check %>%
  group_by(spell_length) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    pct  = n / sum(n) * 100,
    cell = sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", n, pct)
  ) %>%
  select(spell_length, cell) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = spell_length, values_from = cell) %>%
  mutate(Category = "Total (N(%))", Variable = "") %>%
  select(Category, Variable, `One Visit`, `More Than One Visit`)


age_rows <- bind_rows(
  cat_row(check, "age_group", "13-17", "13-17"),
  cat_row(check, "age_group", "18-25", "18-25"),
  cat_row(check, "age_group", "26-45", "26-45"),
  cat_row(check, "age_group", "46-62", "46-62")
) %>% mutate(Category = ifelse(row_number() == 1, "Age in 2021", ""))

sex_rows <- bind_rows(
  cat_row(check, "sex_label", "Female", "Female"),
  cat_row(check, "sex_label", "Male",   "Male")
) %>% mutate(Category = ifelse(row_number() == 1, "Sex", ""))

dx_rows <- bind_rows(
  bin_row(check, "visit1_SMI", "Any SMI"),
  bin_row(check, "visit1_SUD", "Any SUD")
) %>% mutate(Category = ifelse(row_number() == 1, "Diagnosis at first visit", ""))

hx_rows <- bind_rows(
  bin_row(check, "last_year_bh_ip",  "Behavioral Health Related Inpatient Care"),
  bin_row(check, "last_year_bh_ed",  "Behavioral Health Related ED Visit"),
  bin_row(check, "last_year_any_ed", "Any ED Visit")
) %>% mutate(Category = ifelse(row_number() == 1, "Previous year health history", ""))

table_data <- bind_rows(total_row, age_rows, sex_rows, dx_rows, hx_rows)


```

# Repeat number histogram
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(all_states, aes(x = repeat_visits)) + 
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 20, fill="#69b3a2", color="#e9ecef", alpha=0.9) +
  labs(x = "Number of visits")


suppress_20 <- all_states %>% 
  mutate(repeat_visits = if_else(repeat_visits > 20, 20, repeat_visits))

ggplot(suppress_20, aes(x = repeat_visits)) + 
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill="#69b3a2", color="#e9ecef", alpha=0.9) +
  labs(x = "Number of visits")


```
